examples.title: Capitalize First Letter of Each Word (Title Case)
examples.description: Converts a sentence to title case where first letter of each word is uppercase and rest is lowercase
examples.explanation: This method splits the input by whitespace, then for each word: extracts first character using substring(0,1), converts to uppercase; extracts remaining characters using substring(1), converts to lowercase; concatenates both parts. StringBuilder is used for efficient concatenation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the length of input string
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing the result and temporary strings

examples.title: Safe Case-Insensitive String Comparison
examples.description: Demonstrates proper case-insensitive comparison using both approaches
examples.explanation: equalsIgnoreCase() is preferred over manual toLowerCase() comparison as it's optimized and handles null safely. The vowel counter converts to lowercase first then iterates. Turkish locale uses Locale.forLanguageTag("tr") for proper İ/i conversion.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for both methods as each character must be examined
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for the lowercase string creation in countVowels

examples.title: Reverse Case of String
examples.description: Swaps uppercase to lowercase and vice versa while leaving non-letters unchanged
examples.explanation: This method demonstrates using Character wrapper class methods for single character conversion. It creates a char array (mutable), iterates through each character, checks case using isUpperCase/isLowerCase, and converts using toUpperCase/toLowerCase. Returns new String from the modified char array.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - single pass through the string
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) - char array and new String allocation
